saber-alert.name: saber-alert
saber-alert.description: Saber Alert wireless device detection and security alerting platform. Tracks WiFi, BLE, and Bluetooth Classic devices using sensor hardware, clusters randomized MAC addresses into stable identities, builds cross-protocol personas, and generates security alerts for unknown or suspicious devices near monitored areas.
saber-alert.ai_context: This is a multi-tenant IoT security platform. All queries must be scoped by user_key. The star schema centers on dim_users, with alerts_fact as the primary fact table. Devices are tracked through a hierarchy: raw MAC addresses (dim_devices) are grouped into clusters (random_device_clusters) which are unified into personas (dim_personas). Trusted devices are excluded from alerting. Key protocols are wifi, ble, and bt_classic.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique auto-incrementing integer identifier for each activity_feed record

fields.name: user_key
fields.description: Foreign key to dim_users identifying the account that owns this activity_feed record

fields.name: household_id
fields.description: Foreign key to households identifying the household group

fields.name: activity_type
fields.description: Category of the activity event such as device_trusted, alert_acknowledged, or sensor_online

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short human-readable title summarizing the activity event

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed human-readable description of what occurred in this activity event

fields.name: icon
fields.description: Icon identifier for rendering this activity type in the UI

fields.name: related_entity_type
fields.description: Type of the entity this activity relates to, such as device, alert, or sensor

fields.name: related_entity_id
fields.description: Identifier of the related entity (device_key, alert_id, etc.)

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: JSON object containing additional context-specific data for the activity event

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this activity_feed record was first created

fields.name: version_num
fields.description: Alembic database migration version hash identifying the current schema revision

fields.name: user_key
fields.description: Foreign key to dim_users identifying the account that owns this alert

fields.name: sensor_key
fields.description: Foreign key to dim_sensors identifying the sensor that detected the alert

fields.name: alert_type
fields.description: Category of security alert such as unknown_device, proximity_alert, or watched_device

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Alert priority level indicating urgency (high, medium, low)

fields.name: alert_status
fields.description: Current status of the alert (active, acknowledged, dismissed, muted)

fields.name: detected_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the alert-triggering event was first detected by the sensor

fields.name: rssi
fields.description: Received Signal Strength Indicator in dBm measuring proximity of the detected device

fields.name: vendor
fields.description: Hardware vendor name derived from the MAC address OUI prefix lookup

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this alerts_fact record was last modified

fields.name: alert_id
fields.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for each security alert record

fields.name: alert_tier
fields.description: Threat classification tier for the alert based on confidence and recurrence

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Confidence score (0.0-1.0) indicating how certain the system is about the threat assessment

fields.name: mac_address
fields.description: MAC address of the wireless device in standard colon-separated format

fields.name: cluster_id
fields.description: Identifier linking to the random_device_clusters group for this device

fields.name: bssid
fields.description: WiFi access point MAC address (Basic Service Set Identifier) where the device was detected

fields.name: mute_until
fields.description: Timestamp until which this alert is muted and will not generate notifications

fields.name: device_history
fields.description: JSON text containing the historical detection timeline of the alerting device

fields.name: detection_timestamp
fields.description: Precise timestamp of the raw detection event that triggered this alert

fields.name: protocol
fields.description: Wireless protocol of the detected device: wifi, ble, or bt_classic

fields.name: persona_key
fields.description: Foreign key to dim_personas linking the alert to a cross-protocol device identity

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique auto-incrementing integer identifier for each anchors record

fields.name: anchor_uuid
fields.description: UUID uniquely identifying this anchor identity across the system

fields.name: user_key
fields.description: Foreign key to dim_users identifying the account that owns this anchors record

fields.name: protocol
fields.description: Wireless protocol type: wifi, ble, or bt_classic

fields.name: ie_hash_strict
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of WiFi information elements using strict matching criteria for high-confidence fingerprinting

fields.name: ie_hash_loose
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of WiFi information elements using relaxed matching criteria for broader fingerprinting

fields.name: probe_ssid_set
fields.description: JSON array of WiFi network names (SSIDs) this device has probed for, used for fingerprinting

fields.name: first_seen
fields.description: Timestamp when this entity was first detected by any sensor

fields.name: last_seen
fields.description: Timestamp when this entity was most recently detected

fields.name: session_count
fields.description: Number of distinct detection sessions observed for this anchor identity

fields.name: person_id
fields.description: Optional identifier linking this anchor to a known person or persona

fields.name: vendor_oui
fields.description: First three octets of the MAC address identifying the hardware manufacturer (OUI)

fields.name: temporal_profile
fields.description: JSON object describing the typical time-of-day and day-of-week detection patterns

fields.name: is_watched
fields.description: Boolean flag indicating this anchor is on the user watchlist for priority monitoring

fields.name: watchlist_label
fields.description: User-assigned label or name for this watched device

fields.name: watchlist_notes
fields.description: Free-text notes the user has added about this watched device

fields.name: watchlist_added_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the user added this device to the watchlist

fields.name: motion_state
fields.description: Current motion classification: stationary, moving, or unknown

fields.name: motion_confidence
fields.description: Confidence score (0.0-1.0) for the motion state classification

fields.name: ble_fp_strict
fields.description: BLE advertisement fingerprint hash using strict matching for high-confidence identification

fields.name: ble_fp_loose
fields.description: BLE advertisement fingerprint hash using relaxed matching for broader identification

fields.name: ie_strict_match_count
fields.description: Number of device clusters matched using strict IE hash comparison

fields.name: ie_loose_match_count
fields.description: Number of device clusters matched using loose IE hash comparison

fields.name: ssid_only_match_count
fields.description: Number of device clusters matched using only probe SSID set comparison

fields.name: stability_score
fields.description: Numeric score (0-1) indicating how stable and consistent this anchor identity is over time

fields.name: last_stability_computed
fields.description: Timestamp when the stability score was last recalculated

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Bluetooth SIG assigned company identifier number

fields.name: company_name
fields.description: Official company name registered with the Bluetooth SIG

dialects.expression: COUNT(CASE WHEN dim_devices.is_connected = true AND dim_devices.is_current = true THEN 1 END)

business_context.name: platform-overview
business_context.description: Saber Alert is a wireless device detection and security alerting platform that continuously monitors WiFi, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and Bluetooth Classic signals to identify unknown or suspicious devices near a user's property. The system clusters randomized MAC addresses into unified device identities, correlates cross-protocol signals into personas, and generates real-time security alerts when untrusted devices are detected. This semantic layer provides governed access to the detection, clustering, persona, and alerting data that powers the platform's analytics and threat assessment capabilities.

golden_queries.question: How many security alerts were generated per day over the last 30 days?
golden_queries.sql: SELECT DATE(detected_at) AS alert_date, COUNT(*) AS alert_count FROM alerts_fact WHERE detected_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY DATE(detected_at) ORDER BY alert_date DESC

golden_queries.question: What are the top 10 most frequently detected untrusted device clusters?
golden_queries.sql: SELECT cluster_id, device_name, COUNT(*) AS detection_count, MIN(observed) AS first_seen, MAX(last_seen) AS last_seen FROM random_device_clusters WHERE is_trusted = false AND is_auto_trusted = false GROUP BY cluster_id, device_name ORDER BY detection_count DESC LIMIT 10

golden_queries.question: What is the average visit duration by persona movement status?
golden_queries.sql: SELECT p.movement_status, COUNT(v.visit_id) AS visit_count, AVG(v.duration_minutes) AS avg_duration_minutes FROM persona_visits v JOIN dim_personas p ON v.persona_key = p.persona_key WHERE v.duration_minutes IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.movement_status ORDER BY avg_duration_minutes DESC

business_rules.name: trusted-device-exclusion
business_rules.definition: Trusted devices (is_trusted=true or is_auto_trusted=true) are excluded from security alert generation. A device can be trusted manually by the user or auto-trusted when it connects to the user's home WiFi network.

business_rules.name: randomized-mac-clustering
business_rules.definition: Devices using randomized MAC addresses are grouped into clusters based on information element fingerprints (ie_hash_strict/ie_hash_loose), BLE fingerprints, and probe SSID sets. Each cluster represents a single physical device despite changing MAC addresses.

guardrail_filters.name: user-scoped-queries
guardrail_filters.reason: All queries must be scoped to the authenticated user to enforce data isolation between tenants. Never query across users without explicit authorization.

guardrail_filters.name: exclude-infrastructure-devices
guardrail_filters.reason: Infrastructure devices (access points, routers) should be excluded from threat analysis queries by default, as they are known network equipment.

activity-feed.definition: A chronological log of notable system events and user actions within the Saber Alert platform, including device trust changes, alert acknowledgments, sensor status updates, and household membership changes. Each entry includes the activity type, a human-readable title and description, and optional metadata linking to the related entity.

device-cluster.definition: A grouping of wireless MAC addresses that are identified as belonging to the same physical device despite MAC address randomization. Clusters are formed using information element fingerprints (IE hashes), BLE advertisement fingerprints, and probe SSID sets. Each cluster is assigned a stable cluster_id and may be linked to an anchor identity for long-term tracking. Clusters can be trusted manually or auto-trusted when detected on the user's home WiFi network.

persona.definition: A cross-protocol identity that unifies multiple device clusters (WiFi, BLE, Bluetooth Classic) believed to belong to the same physical device or person. Personas aggregate clusters via the persona_members bridge table and track lifecycle status (active, expired), movement patterns, visit history, and trust status. Personas enable the system to reason about a single physical entity even when it presents different identities across wireless protocols.

security-alert.definition: A notification generated when the Saber Alert system detects a potentially suspicious or unknown wireless device near the user's monitored area. Alerts have a type, priority level, confidence score, and tier classification. They are linked to the detecting sensor and may reference a persona. Alerts can be acknowledged, muted, or dismissed by the user, and trusted devices are excluded from alert generation.

checks.label: Every metric field has additive flag
Model description is at least 50 characters
Models with 3+ datasets have at least 3 relationships
Complex models have adequate glossary coverage
